task_completion.description: Extract the SeisComP network inventory from the MySQL database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command connecting to the database (e.g., mysql://sysop:sysop@localhost/seiscomp) or using a tool like scxmldump to export the inventory to an XML/SCML file.

task_completion.description: Redact the latitude, longitude, and elevation for station SANI to 0.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the SCML XML content where <station code="SANI"> and its child <sensorLocation> elements have <latitude>, <longitude>, and <elevation> values explicitly set to 0.0.

task_completion.description: Redact the latitude, longitude, and elevation for station BKB to 0.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the SCML XML content where <station code="BKB"> and its child <sensorLocation> elements have <latitude>, <longitude>, and <elevation> values explicitly set to 0.0.

task_completion.description: Ensure coordinates for other stations (TOLI, GSI, KWP) remain unchanged.
task_completion.visual_evidence: XML content visible in an editor or terminal showing <station> elements for TOLI, GSI, or KWP with their original, non-zero <latitude>, <longitude>, and <elevation> values intact.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting SCML XML file to ~/exports/redacted_inventory.scml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the file saved at /home/ga/exports/redacted_inventory.scml (e.g., via an 'ls' command, a script writing to this path, or the file path visible in a text editor's title bar/status bar).

integrity.description: The agent extracted the data from the actual database rather than hallucinating or manually typing a mock XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands executing a database dump (like scxmldump or a python script connecting to mysql) rather than the agent opening a blank file and pasting a pre-written XML structure.

integrity.description: The agent processed the full inventory rather than just creating a file with only the two redacted stations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal output should show a large XML file with standard SCML headers and multiple other stations (like TOLI, GSI, KWP), not just a tiny snippet.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the file to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a successful save confirmation in the text editor or a terminal command confirming the file exists at ~/exports/redacted_inventory.scml, rather than just printing the XML to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: /home/ga/exports/redacted_inventory.scml. Stations to redact: SANI and BKB (latitude, longitude, and elevation must be exactly 0.0 in both the parent <station> and child <sensorLocation> elements). Stations to keep unchanged: TOLI, GSI, and KWP (must have non-zero coordinates).
